exemplar.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, precise focus on the prescribed title, with a clear conceptual distinction between âcuriosityâ and âaweâ to organize the discussion

Effective integration of both the Natural Sciences and Human Sciences AOKs, supported by specific real-world illustrations (Aurora Borealis, kin-selection, phlogiston, dual-process theory, depression models)
Arguments are logically sequenced and coherent, with explicit consideration of counterclaims and alternative perspectives
Use of historical examples (Four-Element theory, Phlogiston theory) to highlight broader implications for communal inquiry
A clear call to action in the conclusion that emphasizes the open-ended nature of wonder and knowledge acquisition
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally remains surface-level, asserting rather than demonstrating how explanation preserves or erodes wonder
Evaluation of counterarguments is uneven; some are accepted or dismissed without sufficient justification
Conceptual framework sometimes leads to repetition, and transitions between Natural Sciences and Human Sciences sections lack clarity
Several examples (e.g. vaping, kin-selection) are insufficiently linked back to the core question or lack empirical support
The conclusion reiterates definitions rather than offering a sharply focused final insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets many of the descriptors for the 7â8 band by maintaining a sustained focus on the title, linking effectively to two areas of knowledge, and presenting clear, supported arguments with consideration of alternative perspectives. However, recurring surface-level analysis, uneven evaluation of counterclaims, occasional lapses in cohesion and depth, and a somewhat repetitive conclusion constrain the work to the middle of the rubric. These limitations justify awarding 6/10, reflecting solid performance with room for deeper critical engagement and tighter coherence.

annotations.comment: This concluding statement on restrictive frameworks lacks a clear link back to the core question; a more explicit statement on how rationality overrides wonder would improve coherence.
The counterclaim that rational explanations can deepen curiosity adds valuable balance, showing awareness of alternative perspectives within the Human Sciences.
Although counterclaims are acknowledged, the evaluation is uneven; the essay could deepen analysis by weighing the extent to which counterarguments undermine the initial claim.
Attributing all cognitive errors to System 1 oversimplifies the interplay between intuitive and analytical processes; further nuance is needed to avoid generalizing at the expense of depth.

The studentâs interpretation of the title is precise, establishing a clear inquiry into whether knowledge acquisition diminishes both curiosity and awe, which provides a strong foundation for a sustained focus throughout the essay.

The distinction between wonder as âcuriosityâ and wonder as âaweâ creates an effective conceptual framework; however, further depth could be added by exploring how these dimensions interact rather than treating them as wholly separate phenomena.

The introduction of the Northern Lights example sets up a concrete Natural Sciences case, but the claim that explanation does not prompt contemplation lacks supporting evidence; consider elaborating why observers remain focused on emotion rather than understanding.

The initial definition paragraph could be strengthened by situating âwonderâ within TOK concepts such as ways of knowing; this would demonstrate deeper awareness of the courseâs theoretical underpinnings.

Using the Aurora Borealis example demonstrates effective integration of real-world phenomena into the Natural Sciences AOK; the citation adds credibility, though a brief explanation of its relevance to curiosity could strengthen the link.
Although historical examples illustrate complacency, the essay would benefit from contrasting a contemporary case to demonstrate modern relevance.
The conclusion that scientific progress refines rather than destroys curiosity effectively synthesizes previous points and underscores the communal purpose of the Natural Sciences.
Discussing multiple models of depression in the Human Sciences shows nuanced understanding of generalization limits and effectively illustrates how diversity of theories can foster wonder.
A clearer link between the vaping example and the core question of wonder would improve focus; articulate precisely how that research experience influenced your sense of curiosity.
Ensure that each counterargument not only appears but is also explicitly weighed against the main claim to strengthen evaluative rigor.
A brief discussion of how peer review and contemporary scientific practice maintain wonder could bring the historical analysis into a modern context.

The kin selection hypothesis is a fitting Human Sciences example, but the argument that it destroys awe would benefit from empirical support or illustrative cases showing reduced emotional responses over time.

This paragraph asserts that analytical frameworks diminish emotional impact, yet it does not address how individual differences in reception might moderate this effect; consider acknowledging variation among knowers.
A transition sentence linking the Northern Lights discussion to the Human Sciences is missing; a clear bridge would improve the essayâs overall cohesion.
Consider tightening the final paragraphs to avoid redundancy and to leave the reader with a memorable, focused closing thought.

The dual process theory example is well chosen, but the essay stops short of analyzing its broader implications for wonder; consider discussing how simplifying cognition into two systems might itself evoke or limit curiosity.
The analysis of historical complacency using the Four-Element theory effectively illustrates how long-accepted knowledge can stifle inquiry among individual knowers.

The transition to the Phlogiston Theory example is strong, but the essay could enhance its argument by explicitly linking this historical case back to the persistence or revival of wonder in scientific communities.

The personal Extended Essay example enriches the essay but risks anecdotal bias; consider balancing with a broader study or data to demonstrate how individual curiosity scales to communal knowledge-building.
The reflection on implicit biases when engaging with singular Human Sciences perspectives adds critical depth and acknowledges potential pitfalls in knowledge acquisition.
The conclusion reiterates key definitions but becomes repetitive; it would benefit from a concise restatement of main arguments and a stronger final insight into the implications for future knowers.
This closing statement effectively emphasizes the open-ended nature of wonder and knowledge, providing a clear call to action for knowers to maintain curiosity.
Overall, the essayâs structure is logical, but adding signposting at key transitions would guide the reader through complex arguments more smoothly.

described by levels
1â5 or is not a

on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
